_comment: Error registry — single source of truth for every failure mode. Consumed by scripts/errors.py (exit codes), scripts/notify.py (auto-filed issues), scripts/apply_config.py (renders docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md), and fleet health checks. Add codes here first; never invent ad-hoc exit codes.

CFG-001.user_impact: None — installed plugins keep working from the last published state.
CFG-001.admin_fix: Restore the file (`git checkout main -- marketplace.config.yml`) and re-run `python3 scripts/apply_config.py`.

CFG-002.meaning: marketplace.config.yml failed to parse (strict YAML subset: 2-space indent, no tabs, no anchors, no flow collections)
CFG-002.user_impact: None — nothing is rendered or published until the config parses.
CFG-002.admin_fix: Open the file at the reported line and fix the syntax. The parser tells you the exact file:line. Keep to `key: value`, nested 2-space blocks, and `- item` lists.

CFG-003.meaning: marketplace.config.yml has an unknown key or an invalid value for a known key
CFG-003.user_impact: None — nothing is rendered or published until the config validates.
CFG-003.admin_fix: Fix the reported key. Valid keys and defaults are documented as comments inside marketplace.config.yml itself.

CFG-004.meaning: A rendered document has corrupted config markers (unbalanced or unknown <!-- cfg:... --> / <!-- gen:... --> pairs)
CFG-004.user_impact: None — docs may look stale but plugins are unaffected.
CFG-004.admin_fix: Open the reported file:line and restore the marker pair (see docs/AUTHORING.md, 'Config markers'). Then re-run `python3 scripts/apply_config.py --check`.

CFG-005.meaning: Rendered files drifted from marketplace.config.yml (someone hand-edited a generated value)
CFG-005.user_impact: None — CI blocks the drifting change from merging.
CFG-005.admin_fix: Run `python3 scripts/apply_config.py` and commit the result. Edit marketplace.config.yml (the source), never the rendered value.

CFG-006.meaning: A denylisted telemetry key (OTEL_LOG_*) appeared in config or rendered output — logs pipelines are never allowed, only metrics
CFG-006.user_impact: None — the render is blocked before anything ships.
CFG-006.admin_fix: Remove the OTEL_LOG_* reference from marketplace.config.yml or the template that produced it. This marketplace ships metrics-only telemetry by design (see docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md).

GATE-001.meaning: Manifest validation failed (marketplace.json / plugin.json schema or cross-consistency)
GATE-001.user_impact: None — the broken change cannot merge.
GATE-001.admin_fix: Run `python3 scripts/validate.py` locally and fix every finding it lists.

GATE-002.meaning: Risk lint failed (dangerous pattern, off-allowlist network destination, or under-declared risk tier)
GATE-002.user_impact: None — the risky change cannot merge.
GATE-002.admin_fix: Run `python3 scripts/risk_lint.py` locally; read risk-report.json. Fix the finding or, for a legitimate new domain, add it to security.network_allowlist in marketplace.config.yml.

GATE-003.meaning: Smoke test failed (broken frontmatter, missing referenced file, or invalid plugin JSON config)
GATE-003.user_impact: None — the broken plugin cannot merge.
GATE-003.admin_fix: Run `python3 scripts/smoke_test.py` locally and fix the listed structural problems.

GATE-004.meaning: Version gate failed (plugin files changed without a semver bump + CHANGELOG entry)
GATE-004.admin_fix: Bump `version` in the plugin's plugin.json AND its marketplace.json entry (keep identical), and add a `## [x.y.z]` entry to the plugin's CHANGELOG.md.

GATE-005.meaning: Claude security review found critical issues (prompt injection, exfiltration, or hidden instructions)
GATE-005.user_impact: None — the flagged change cannot merge.
GATE-005.admin_fix: Download the llm-review-report artifact from the PR run, read the findings, and fix or reject the submission.

BUILD-001.meaning: A generated output failed to build (catalog, site, or TROUBLESHOOTING)
BUILD-001.user_impact: None — users keep the last published catalog.
BUILD-001.admin_fix: Run `python3 scripts/build_catalog.py && python3 scripts/build_site.py` locally and read the traceback.

NOTIFY-001.meaning: A notification could not be delivered (GitHub issue filing or Slack webhook failed) — notify.py never blocks a pipeline, so this is informational
NOTIFY-001.admin_fix: Check `gh auth status` / GITHUB_TOKEN permissions and, if Slack is enabled, the SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL secret. Notifications resume on the next event.

CI-001.meaning: Post-merge automation failed — catalog, site, scorecards, or rendered files were not refreshed after a merge
CI-001.user_impact: Users may see a slightly stale catalog; installed plugins are unaffected.
CI-001.admin_fix: Open the failed post-merge run in GitHub Actions, read the failing step, fix, then re-run the workflow (`gh run rerun <id>`).

CI-002.meaning: GitHub Pages deploy failed after retry (the catalog site did not update)
CI-002.user_impact: The browsable catalog site is stale; installs via /plugin still work.
CI-002.admin_fix: Re-run the pages workflow (`gh workflow run pages.yml`). If it keeps failing, confirm Pages is enabled: Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions.
